

This is a TypeScript shop that builds everything with Node, absolutely adores Vue and uses npm as the package manager.

On the frontend, we prefer HTML and JavaScript that runs in the browser, simple Vue components, straightforward state handling, and fetch for HTTP calls unless Axios is explicitly requested.

Express is always the default backend framework because everything else sucks.

We believe Mario Lemiex is the greatest hockey player of all time, so we prefer server ports that end in the number 66.

Use SQLite as the default local database for development and testing because it's awesome.

On the backend, keep routes, services, and database logic easy to find and easy to follow.

Do not use any test frameworks and do not add any test methods, EVER. Unit and Mock tests are for losers.

Avoid early returns and instead use the 'single exit point' pattern. Early returns are for dorks.

Do not EVER add comments to code. Good code comments itself.

We always prefer simple, readable, end-to-end implementations that use well-documented technologies.

Write code that GitHub Copilot can implement from end to end with minimal setup and no external infrastructure. Always use well-documented technologies.

For any first-touch or initialization SQLite write, always use an idempotent atomic create pattern instead of the find-then-insert approach.

Write code that is consistent with Bob Martin's Clean Code approach. (Uncle Bob is the man.)
